methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $50M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Orbital Marine Power is a Growth-stage private company in the Ocean & Maritime sector. The company has raised $50M+ to date. The comparable universe is sparse, consisting of one identified private peer (Ocean Infinity) and no public comparables, reflecting the early-stage nature of frontier technology commercialization in marine energy.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method given limited market data. The Stage-Progression approach uses total capital raised ($50M) as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $60M. This method assumes standard progression mechanics but is constrained by the absence of revenue multiples, EBITDA benchmarks, or transaction precedent data for direct cross-checking.

The triangulated range spans $51M (low) to $69M (median $60M). The narrow range reflects methodology limitation rather than conviction—a single-method analysis provides directional guidance only. Key uncertainties include: commercialization timeline and unit economics for ocean-based power systems; the relevance of Ocean Infinity as a peer given potential business model divergence; and whether $50M+ represents fully deployed or committed capital. A more robust valuation would require either expanded comparable data, revenue or customer traction metrics, or forward-looking cash flow assumptions. Current analysis supports a $51M–$69M range pending additional information.
